candidates.evidence_text: Manchester has never stood still. It is a city in constant motion. A place that has looked to shape and adapt to economic change. The strapline we’re carrying into MIPIM this week says it all: Manchester: Breaking Boundaries, Building Tomorrow Alongside this strapline we will be using a refreshed Invest Manchester identity across all our activity. We have made this change to reflect the city region’s ambitions and to bring together MIDAS and Marketing Manchester’s promotion activity. We want the world to be as excited Invest Manchester will be the single market facing identity for all our business investment work. Whether that is real estate, foreign direct investment, sector and place development, or international partnerships. Crucially, it will provide us with a collaborative platform, alon The investmanchester.co.uk website is live. We will work together with partners as we build content over the next six months, roll out our brand identity, and bring forward new campaigns We’re at a pivotal moment for the city region. Greater Manchester has built something remarkable. Average annual economic growth of 3.1% for the last ten years, over double the national average; 100,000 new homes delivered; an integrated public transport system; and population gr Over the last decade, we’ve attracted more foreign direct investment than any other UK regional city. A revised planning In total, HPARK will deliver 2.1 million sq ft of design-and-build space, with flexibility to accommodate a range of occupiers, including larger combined units. Buildings will be delivered to a high specification, including up to 18m eaves heights, Grade A office space, generous The development targets BREEAM ‘Excellent’ and an EPC A rating, with sustainability features including solar PV panels, EV charging infrastructure and air source heat pumps

candidates.lead: Bafta and Emmy-nominated composer Ilan Eshkeri joins forces with the European Space Agency for a dazzling audio-visual experience at Aviva Studios this summer Space Station Earth invites audiences to see through the eyes of an astronaut, exploring how witnessing the Earth from sp
candidates.evidence_text: Bafta and Emmy-nominated composer Ilan Eshkeri joins forces with the European Space Agency for a dazzling audio-visual experience at Aviva Studios this summer Space Station Earth invites audiences to see through the eyes of an astronaut, exploring how witnessing the Earth from space reshapes human perspective Eshkeri’s album is performed live on synthesisers with orchestra and choir, unfolding as a continuous musical work that moves from moments of near-stillness to overwhelming scale The concert is presented alongside a film written and directed by Eshkeri, created from footage captured by astronauts aboard the International Space Station and imagery filmed at European Space Agency locations Projected across three large-scale screens, the film unfolds in dialogue with the music, framing the band and orchestra and extending the music’s sense of scale and emotion, moving between vast planetary imagery and moments of intimate detail The work engages with the Overview Effect — the profound shift in perception that comes from seeing Earth from space — allowing this perspective to be encountered through the unfolding of the music

candidates.evidence_text: Salford’s iconic Lowry stage will be transformed into the fiery dancefloors of Spain this June as it hosts a summer Flamenco Festival Featuring two very different shows, each entirely authentic in its own unique style, the festival will showcase the breadth and diversity of the beloved traditional art form First, with performances on Sat 13 Jun and Sun 14 Jun, acclaimed dancer and choreographer Olga Pericet presents Baile Sonoro , her new flamenco dance production blending tradition and innovation Baile Sonoro combines the strength and subtlety of Olga’s incredible movement with live guitar and singing to create a mesmerising, intimate and intense dance-concert experience Then, on Tue 30 Jun, Ballet Flamenco de Andalucía’s Tierra Bendita: Blessed Land journeys through the most striking corner of Andalusia, the birthplace of flamenco. Through a vibrant selection of iconic music and dances, this stunning show celebrates the passion, history and beau Under the direction of Patricia Guerrero, winner of the National Dance Award of Spain 2021, Tierra Bendita merges 18th-century stage elements with contemporary ones in a striking balance of tradition and modernity

candidates.evidence_text: The renowned Kantos Chamber Choir celebrates its 10th anniversary by joining forces with two fellow Mancunian musical institutions for a special one-off show this June On Sat 20 Jun, Hope on Earth will see Kantos collaborate with the Royal Northern College of Music (RNCM) Chamber Choir and the University of Manchester Chamber Choir for a stirring evening of music that celebrates Kantos’ legacy so far, and the future of choral music in the North The evening’s programme will be crafted around powerful songs of protest, change and hope. In true Kantos style, brand-new pop arrangements will sit alongside seminal choral masterpieces; from Aaron Copland to Bob Dylan, and Arnold Schoenberg to Louis Armstrong Tallis’ monumental Spem in Alium sits at the heart of the performance, with audiences invited to experience this 40-part masterpiece in surround sound, in the glorious acoustic and dramatic backdrop of Manchester Monastery After the show, all guests are invited to continue the celebrations with a post-concert toast to a decade of Kantos For more information and to book tickets, click the button below

candidates.evidence_text: Until 3 October 2026 View dates and times Date Time Session Features 16 May 2026 10:00 am-12:00 pm 03 Oct 2026 10:00 am-12:00 pm Additional information Always double check opening hours with the venue before making a special visit No matter how well you think you know Didsbury , there are hidden histories, fascinating buildings, and lots of local personalities that you almost certainly won’t know on this walking tour with Jonathan Schofield Tours It’s not hard to see why this Manchester suburb is so sought after. It blends village charm with urban convenience and a vibrant community atmosphere. It boasts gorgeous historic buildings alongside a whole network of trendy, independent hospitality and retail businesses. And it Hidden beneath the surface of all this are stories. Lots and lots of stories. From screen-worthy romances to notorious murderers via whole potato-swallowers and eccentric aldermen, Didsbury has the lot. All will be revealed by Jonathan Schofield Tours This tour takes you from the old station area close to the lovely Carnegie library down to Fletcher Moss and then onto The Towers. Along the way you’ll learn about many remarkable buildings, including some shining examples of Edwardian and Victorian architecture as well as a few Your knowledgeable and experienced tour guide will also reveal a rich tapestry of history lurking beneath these places, some surprising facts, and – being a Jonathan Schofield Tours – probably a few gags, too

candidates.evidence_text: From years spent languishing in post-industrial decline to being crowned one of ‘the coolest neighbourhoods in the world’ Until 11 July 2026 View dates and times Date Time Session Features 11 Apr 2026 10:30 am-12:30 pm 11 Jul 2026 10:30 am-12:30 pm Additional information Always double check opening hours with the venue before making a special visit Is there any place in the UK that has changed so quickly and so utterly as Ancoats ? From years spent languishing in post-industrial decline to being crowned one of ‘the coolest neighbourhoods in the world’ in the blink of an eye. What’s the story here? Find out with Jonathan Schofield Tours, with whom you’ll track Ancoats’ changes by strolling its streets, crossing its canals, and taking a look inside one of its most fascinating buildings Starting at Cutting Room Square, your expert tour guide will reveal the layers of history that make Ancoats special, from its place in establishing Manchester’s reputation as ‘Cottonopolis’ to the notorious street gangs, or ‘scuttlers’, who once patrolled and fought here But this winsome stroll is also about the urban regeneration and cultural energy of Ancoats today, which boasts everything from grassroots theatres to Manchester’s only Michelin-starred restaurant

candidates.evidence_text: Manchester Central Library , Manchester Until 20 December 2026 Additional information Always double check opening hours with the venue before making a special visit Manchester has always been a city with stories to tell. This guided walking tour will bring those stories to life. Led by a local expert, the experience is set to be a lively introduction to the people, places and ideas that have shaped one of the UK’s most influential cities The tour will begin at Manchester Central Library, that impossible to miss, grand circular landmark on St Peter’s Square. The neoclassical design has made it one of the city’s most-loved buildings, holding thousands of stories in its walls and on its shelves. The square itself ha From here, the tour takes you through the city’s heart, down the colourful streets of the Gay Village and the calming Sackville Gardens. It is here that you will see the monuments that honour Manchester’s role in the fight for equality and the city’s pioneering LGBTQ+ community a This guided tour is filled with monuments and tales from many moons ago. The Medieval Quarter is the perfect place to soak in all that history. It is here where you will see the oldest public library in the English-speaking world, Chetham’s Library . Housed in a beautiful sandsto During all this walking, your guide will point out local street art, hidden corners and stories you’d easily miss on your own. Tales of suffragettes, reformers, musicians and everyday Mancunians who’ve all left their mark on the city in their own way

This mischievously-named comedy show is here to make the uncomfortable, comfortable. A disabled-led comedy night that gleefully tramples over polite conversation and replaces it with punchlines so sharp and off the cuff, The evening is curated and hosted by Wheels and the Legman, entertainers that have built a reputation for creating spaces where disability isn’t treated as an aside but is, instead, the jumping off point for fearless, boundary-pushing comedy. Their mission is simple: dismantle a The line-up brings together a clutch of comedians who know all too well how to humorously weaponise personal experience. Dawn Bailey, Mike Keenan, Anthony Johnston, Paul Campbell and Jenny Hart will all be performing, each delivering their own take on the absurdities of everyday The best stand-up has always thrived on discomfort, and this night embraces that tradition three-fold. The Manchester Flower Festival Manchester city centre Saturday 23 – Monday 25 May 2026 www.mcrflowerfest.com Facebook.com/mcrflowerfest Instagram: @mcrflowerfest TikTok: mcrflowerfest . THE MANCHESTER FLOWER FESTIVAL RETURNS FOR 2026 CELEBRATING ‘GREEN MANCHESTER’ . Returning this May, the festival brings immersive gardens, floral installations and a city-wide celebration of urban nature to Manchester city centre. . Manchester will burst into life this May as The Manchester Flower Festival returns from Saturday 23 to Monday 25 May, transforming the city centre into a vibrant celebration of flowers, green spaces and urban nature.. Celebrating its ninth year as Manchester’s most Instagrammable event, The Manchester Flower Festival is set to ‘take root’ on St Ann’s Square and King Street over the late May Bank Holiday weekend, inviting visitors to explore a greener side to the city through spectacular displays, outdoor dining and a bloomin’ brilliant programme of events.. This year’s theme, Green Manchester highlights Manchester’s expanding green spaces and vision for a more sustainable city, while providing a vibrant, free festival for citygoers to celebrate the Bank Holiday weekend in full bloom.. At the heart of this year’s festival will be five large-scale headline floral and horticultural displays, each inspired by green initiatives including the Piccadilly Gardens development, Castlefield Viaduct, Manchester’s urban green spaces and the beehives of The Royal Exchange Manchester.. Organised by Manchester City Centre BID on behalf of city centre businesses, the festival has become a firm favourite in Manchester’s cultural calendar, filled with exciting events and activities that draw tens of thousands of visitors each year.. Alongside the striking floral trail, visitors can dig a little deeper with a packed programme of talks, tours and workshops, as well as family-friendly fun with the Conker Crew. King Street will also be blooming with outdoor dining, with venues including Gail’s Bakery, El Gato Negro

What is Pedddle? . Blog . Contact Us . Join Pedddle . The Market Success Guide . Login . Search . Search for: . Northern Quarter Makers Market. Second Sunday of every month Manchester, M4 5JD. Taking place on the second Sunday of every month in the heart of Manchester, the Northern Quarter Makers Market is a great market for an independent, edgy city vibe, with 95 stallholders showcasing their wares. . . . Details. Favourite Address Oak Street Manchester M4 5JD . Date Second Sunday of every month Time 11:00am - 5:00pm Next Markets 14th Jun 2026 , 12th Jul 2026 , 9th Aug 2026 , 13th Sep 2026 , 11th Oct 2026 , 8th Nov 2026 , 13th Dec 2026 Organiser The Makers Market Social Website themakersmarket.co.uk/pages/northern-quarter-makers-market Accessibility Generally a flat market, wheelchair and pushchair accessible, but being in central Manchester please expect kerbs and the usual pedestrian/traffic. Parking Nearby city centre car parks available. We recommend arriving on public transport; the nearest tram stop is Shudehill (4 min walk), by Bus please use Shudehill Interchange (4 min walk), or if arriving by train, this market is just a 14 minute walk from Piccadilly (Cycle racks available). Facilities Wheelchair Accessible All Outdoor Seating Available Dog Friendly Free Entry Markets in Manchester , North West , Greater Manchester . The Northern Quarter Makers Market makes Manchester come alive with passionate creatives that are eager to share and inspire visitors with their award-winning food and drink, exhibiting awesome art, modern craft and vintage and live music. Northern Quarter Makers Market showcases around 95 stallholders, and continues to promise the creme de la creme when it comes to artisan traders in Manchester and the North West. A great place to visit if you want to shop independent in the city. They say ‘a weekend well spent brings a week of content’… so we’ll see you the in NQ!. Share this:. candidates.evidence_text: Altrincham Market and market house, part restaurant, part market, part town square. Food, drink, shopping and more Altrincham ‪Market House. Eat, meet, drink, shop, watch, talk, listen, laugh. Come in, be yourself and if you like it bring your friends! Altrincham’s charter dates back to 1290, it’s location at the intersection of the Cheshire farm belt and the commercial commuter zone of Manchester means the town has a long and important history. Altrincham market is now at the heart of the regeneration of ‘the modern market tow The covered market hosts regional food producers, vintage fashion and furniture, home & garden and contemporary craft on a Tuesday, Friday, Saturday and Sunday Altrincham Market House Opening Hours Tues-Sat : 9am-10pm Sun : 9am-6pm Mon : Closed The Outdoor Market (Shopping) Tues: 8am-3pm Fri: 8am-3pm Sat: 8am-4pm Sun: 10am-4pm Altrincham Market House is home to award-winning independent quality food operators. These include the following: Honest Crust – wood fired pizza “among the country’s best”. Tender Cow who serve rare breed flat iron steak and chips. Jack in the Box selling fantastic craft beers. Bowlee is back - car boot sale and market returns for new season . . . Bowlee is back - car boot sale and market returns for new season . The new car boot season at Bowlee Community Park in Middleton begins on Sunday, 5 April 2026. Published: 3 March 2026 . . Bowlee Car Boot Sale and Market – one of the most popular in Greater Manchester, attracting thousands of visitors every week, returns for the new season on Sunday, 5 April 2026.. Organised by Rochdale Borough Council, the 2026 season at Bowlee Community Park in Middleton begins with an Easter bank holiday double on Sunday, 5 April 2026 and Monday, 6 April 2026 and then runs on Sundays and bank holiday Mondays right through until 11 October 2026 (excluding Sunday, 3 May 2026). . Sellers do not need to pre-book, and for the 8th year in a row prices have been frozen at £15 for cars, £20 for small vans/people carriers/4X4s and £25 for large vans and above. For buyers, there are parking spaces for more than 1,200 cars at £2.50 per vehicle.. Bowlee Car Boot Sale and Market is returning for its 16th year.. . ‘One of the busiest ever’. The event, now in its 16th year, takes a break on Sunday, 3 May 2026 to make way for Tour de Manc – the annual Greater Manchester charity bike ride that sets off from Bowlee Park with the choice of a 60-kilometre, 100-kilometre, 165-kilometre mile or 201-kilometre ride.. Up to 31 sales will take place this year and on 2 of the sales, parking for buyers will be reduced to £1. In response to customer feedback, if bad weather is forecast, a decision will be announced by 3pm the day before the sale, instead of the previous 6pm.. The event is one of the most popular in Greater Manchester, attracting thousands of visitors every week.. . Councillor Tricia Ayrton, cabinet member for climate change and environment, said:. “I am pleased this great event is returning to Bowlee Community Park for another year. We have an amazing following at Bowlee and last year was one of the busiest ever. Bargain hunters can dig out great deals and one-off items, and of course you can sell your own things too. People are a lot more conscious about recycling and re-selling now, so car boot sales are a great way of extending the life of items and reducing waste.”. View all the new dates and more details about the events . Bowlee Community Park is off Heywood Old Road (A6045), Middleton M24 4SB with easy access from the M60 (junction 19). Find Bowlee Community Park on Google Maps . Social media. Follow @BowleeCarBoot on Facebook . Search #BowleeCarBoot on Instagram . Related content. Councillor Tricia Ayrton . candidates.evidence_text: Manchester Opera House is one of the largest theatres in Manchester with a capacity of 1920 seats. The Opera House opened in 1912 and is one of Manchester’s premier venues. Hosting musicals, dance, opera, comedy, concerts and the family favourite, the Christmas pantomime, the Man Some of the biggest productions have been performed on stage at the Manchester Opera House including The Phantom of the Opera Manchester Opera House has more exciting shows coming up including Back To The Future The Musical, The Full Monty, Just For One Day - The Live Aid Musical, The Curious Incident of the Dog in the Night-Time, The Mousetrap, The Choir of Man, Mean Girls, Legally Blonde, Something Ro There are a number of Q-Park and NCP car parks within easy walking distance from the theatre. The closest car park is Spinningfields NCP Alternatively you can park at other car parks in Manchester, with 15% off through Q-Park - click here for more info There is accessible parking at NCP Spinningfields and NCP Great Northern which are located close by to the theatre

L In 2018, The O2 Victoria Warehouse became part of Academy Music Group, the UK’s leading owner and operator of music venues. It hosts conferences, meetings, events, award shows, and concerts, including BBC 6 Music Festival, The Warehouse project, and renowned artists such as Fatbo The O2 Victoria Warehouse Manchester is one of the UK’s most ambitious and forward-thinking event venues. Clients include Manchester United, Audi, Coco-Cola, Microsoft, and Netflix to name a few.
